info.name: rfq_manager_ms — Test Suite V1.1 (Revised)
info.description:
Complete test suite for the RFQ Manager Microservice — V1.1 Revised.

**Improvements over V1.0:**
- Namespaced variables: each RFQ context has its own stage IDs (rfq1_stage_1_id, cancel_stage_1_id, etc.)
- Post-state assertions: every mutation verifies side effects, not just status codes
- Payload notes: tricky fields documented inline
- Starting state: each folder describes its prerequisites
- Schema vs business rule: negative tests specify which layer rejects

**How to use:**
1. Import this collection + environment into Postman
2. Server running + DB seeded (2 workflows, 4 rules)
3. Run '0 — Setup' first
4. Run folders in order (each builds on previous state)

**Variable strategy:**
- Template IDs: tpl_stage_1 through tpl_stage_11 (from workflow, never change)
- RFQ 1 stages: rfq1_s1_id through rfq1_s11_id
- Cancel test: cancel_rfq_id, cancel_s1_id, cancel_s2_id
- Award test: award_rfq_id
- Demo: demo_rfq_id, demo_s1 through demo_s11

item.name: 0 — Setup (Run First)
item.description:
**Objective:** Populate template-level variables from seeded data.
**Starting state:** Fresh DB with seed.py executed.
**Variables produced:** workflow_long_id, workflow_short_id, tpl_stage_1 through tpl_stage_11, tpl_short_stage_1 through tpl_short_stage_5.

item.name: GET GHI-LONG Detail → Save Template Stage IDs

exec: // Save TEMPLATE stage IDs — these are for skip_stages and stage_overrides
// NOTE: These are NOT the same as RFQ stage IDs. Template IDs are used only in POST /rfqs payloads.
console.log('Template stage IDs saved: tpl_stage_1 through tpl_stage_11');

item.name: GET GHI-SHORT Detail → Save Short Template IDs

exec: console.log('Short template stage IDs saved: tpl_short_stage_1 through tpl_short_stage_5');

item.name: 1 — Core Lifecycle [HIGH]
item.description:
**Objective:** Prove end-to-end RFQ creation, stage generation, advancement, and progress tracking.
**Starting state:** Setup completed. No RFQs exist yet.
**Variables produced:** rfq1_id, rfq1_code, rfq1_s1_id through rfq1_s11_id.
**This folder creates RFQ #1 and advances through stages 1-2.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "HP Separator Package — Offshore Platform Alpha",
  "client": "Saudi Aramco",
  "deadline": "2025-08-15",
  "owner": "Proposals Team A",
  "workflow_id": "{{workflow_long_id}}",
  "industry": "Oil & Gas — Upstream",
  "country": "Saudi Arabia",
  "priority": "critical",
  "description": "High-pressure separator package for offshore platform. 3 vessels, ASME VIII Div 1.",
  "code_prefix": "IF"
}

exec: // - workflow_id: UUID of the workflow (from Setup). Required.
// - code_prefix: 'IF' or 'IB'. Controls rfq_code prefix. Default: 'IF'.
// - priority: must be exactly 'normal' or 'critical' (Literal enum).
pm.test('Status is In preparation', () => pm.expect(rfq.status).to.eql('In preparation'));

item.name: [STAGES] TC-LC-02: List stages → 11 generated, save IDs

exec: pm.test('Stage 1 is In Progress', () => pm.expect(stages[0].status).to.eql('In Progress'));
pm.test('Stage 2 is Not started', () => pm.expect(stages[1].status).to.eql('Not started'));
// Save RFQ1 stage IDs — namespaced to avoid collision with other RFQs
console.log('RFQ1 stage IDs saved: rfq1_s1_id through rfq1_s11_id');

item.name: [DETAIL] TC-LC-03: Get RFQ detail → all fields populated

exec: pm.test('Client is Saudi Aramco', () => pm.expect(rfq.client).to.eql('Saudi Aramco'));
pm.test('Country is Saudi Arabia', () => pm.expect(rfq.country).to.eql('Saudi Arabia'));

item.name: [DETAIL] TC-LC-04: Get stage 1 detail → empty children

exec: pm.test('Status is In Progress', () => pm.expect(s.status).to.eql('In Progress'));

item.name: [VERIFY] TC-LC-06: Verify stage 2 auto-activated + RFQ state

exec: // Post-state: verify RFQ current_stage moved to stage 2
pm.test('RFQ still In preparation', () => pm.expect(rfq.status).to.eql('In preparation'));

item.name: [UPDATE] TC-LC-07: Fill mandatory field for stage 2

exec: // NOTE: captured_data is a JSONB field. You send the entire object.
pm.test('Status still In Progress (not auto-advanced)', () => pm.expect(pm.response.json().status).to.eql('In Progress'));
// Post-state: verify RFQ progress and current stage moved to 3

item.name: [LIST] TC-LC-09: List RFQs with filters + pagination

exec: pm.test('Has pagination: data, total, page, size', () => {

item.name: 2 — Business Rule Enforcement [HIGH]
item.description:
**Objective:** Prove the system correctly REJECTS invalid operations.
**Starting state:** RFQ #1 exists with stages 1-2 completed, stage 3 is current (In Progress).
**Variables used:** rfq1_id, rfq1_s3_id, rfq1_s4_id, workflow_long_id.
**Note:** This folder advances stage 3 (no mandatory) then tests blockers + mandatory on stage 4.

item.name: [ADVANCE] TC-BR-00: Advance stage 3 (no mandatory) → setup for stage 4 tests

exec: // Post-state: confirm stage 4 is now active

request.description:
Stage 4 (Preliminary design) requires 'design_approved' in captured_data.
Expected failure: BUSINESS RULE (422) — mandatory fields guard, not schema validation.

exec: // EXPECTED FAILURE: Business rule — mandatory field 'design_approved' missing from captured_data
// This is NOT a schema error. The request format is valid. The business logic rejects it.
pm.test('Status 422 — business rule: mandatory fields missing', () => pm.response.to.have.status(422));
// POST-STATE: verify stage 4 is UNCHANGED (still In Progress, not corrupted)
pm.test('Stage 4 still In Progress (no side effect)', () => pm.expect(res.json().status).to.eql('In Progress'));
// NOTE: blocker_status must be exactly 'Blocked' or 'Resolved' (Literal enum).
pm.test('Status still In Progress (blocker is orthogonal)', () => pm.expect(pm.response.json().status).to.eql('In Progress'));

request.description: Expected failure: BUSINESS RULE (409 Conflict) — blocker prevents advancement.

exec: // EXPECTED FAILURE: Business rule — stage is blocked
// POST-STATE: verify no side effects — stage and RFQ unchanged
pm.test('Stage 4 still In Progress (not corrupted)', () => pm.expect(res.json().status).to.eql('In Progress'));

item.name: [ADVANCE] TC-BR-05: Advance stage 4 → success after recovery

exec: // EXPECTED FAILURE: SCHEMA validation — Pydantic Literal rejects 'urgent'
// POST-STATE: no RFQ created (verify count unchanged is impractical, but no new ID returned)
// EXPECTED FAILURE: SCHEMA — Literal enum rejects 'Pending'
// Valid: Draft, In preparation, Submitted, Awarded, Lost, Cancelled
pm.test('RFQ status unchanged (In preparation)', () => pm.expect(res.json().status).to.eql('In preparation'));
// EXPECTED FAILURE: NOT FOUND — workflow UUID doesn't exist in DB

item.name: 3A — Data Integrity / Code Generation [HIGH]
item.description:
**Objective:** Prove rfq_code auto-increments and dual prefix support.
**Starting state:** RFQ #1 exists (IF-0001 or similar).
**Creates:** RFQ #2 (IF prefix), RFQ #3 (IB prefix).

item.name: [CODE] TC-DI-02: Create RFQ with IB prefix → separate sequence
3B — Data Integrity / Subtask Rollup [HIGH]
item.description:
**Objective:** Prove subtask → stage progress rollup and soft delete behavior.
**Starting state:** RFQ #1 at stage 5 (In Progress after folder 2 advanced stage 4).
**Variables used:** rfq1_id, rfq1_s5_id.
**Variables produced:** sub1_id, sub2_id.

exec: // NOTE: Subtask creation uses 'name' (not 'title'). assigned_to and due_date are optional.
// NOTE: Subtask update uses PATCH. Status must be 'Open', 'In progress', or 'Done' (Literal).
// POST-STATE: stage progress should be average = (100 + 0) / 2 = 50

item.name: [SOFTDELETE] TC-DI-06: Delete subtask 2 → stage recalculates to 100
[VERIFY] TC-DI-07: Stage progress = 100, deleted subtask gone
3C — Data Integrity / Deadline [MEDIUM]
item.description:
**Objective:** Prove deadline change recalculates uncompleted stage dates.
**Starting state:** RFQ #1 with some stages completed.
**Uses:** rfq1_id.

item.name: [DEADLINE] TC-DI-08: Change deadline → verify dates shift
[VERIFY] TC-DI-09: Last stage end = new deadline, completed untouched
4 — Flexibility [MEDIUM]
item.description:
**Objective:** Prove multiple workflows, skip_stages, and stage_overrides.
**Starting state:** Setup completed (template IDs available).
**Each scenario creates a FRESH RFQ.
**IMPORTANT PAYLOAD NOTES:**
- skip_stages expects an array of TEMPLATE stage UUIDs (from tpl_stage_X variables), NOT order numbers
- stage_overrides[].stage_template_id is the template UUID, NOT a stage instance ID

item.name: [SHORT] TC-FL-01: Create RFQ with GHI-SHORT → 5 stages

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Repeat Order — Standard Heat Exchanger",
  "client": "Saudi Aramco",
  "deadline": "2025-07-30",
  "owner": "Quick Proposals Team",
  "workflow_id": "{{workflow_short_id}}"
}

item.name: [SKIP] TC-FL-03: Create with skip_stages (skip template stages 3 and 6)

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Custom Workflow — No Pre-bid or Vendor Inquiry",
  "client": "SABIC",
  "deadline": "2025-10-01",
  "owner": "Proposals Team A",
  "workflow_id": "{{workflow_long_id}}",
  "skip_stages": ["{{tpl_stage_3}}", "{{tpl_stage_6}}"]
}

exec: // NOTE: skip_stages expects TEMPLATE stage UUIDs (tpl_stage_X from Setup).
// These are NOT RFQ stage instance IDs. NOT order numbers like 3 or 6.
// EXPECTED FAILURE: BUSINESS RULE (400) — cannot create RFQ with zero stages
// This is NOT a schema error. The array is valid. The business logic rejects zero remaining stages.
pm.test('Status 400 — business rule: zero stages', () => pm.response.to.have.status(400));
// NOTE: stage_overrides[].stage_template_id is the TEMPLATE UUID, NOT a stage instance ID.
// assigned_team is free text — any string.
pm.test('Stage 7 = Senior Estimation Lead', () => pm.expect(s7.assigned_team).to.eql('Senior Estimation Lead'));
pm.test('Stage count = 11 (overrides dont change count)', () => pm.expect(stages.length).to.eql(11));

item.name: 5 — Supporting Resources [MEDIUM]
item.description:
**Objective:** Prove notes, reminders, workflows, stats, and analytics.
**Starting state:** Multiple RFQs exist from previous folders.
**Uses:** rfq1_id, rfq1_s5_id for notes/reminders.

item.name: 6 — Interruption & State Transitions [HIGH]
item.description:
**Objective:** Prove terminal state freezing, cancellation, award, and state permanence.
**Each scenario creates a FRESH RFQ to avoid state contamination.
**Variables:** cancel_rfq_id, award_rfq_id, lost_rfq_id (all namespaced).

item.name: [FRESH RFQ] TC-IT-01: Create RFQ for cancellation test

exec: // EXPECTED FAILURE: CONFLICT — terminal state is permanent

exec: // EXPECTED FAILURE: CONFLICT — terminal-to-terminal transition blocked

item.name: [APPEND] TC-IT-09: Add note to completed stage (post-clarification)

exec: pm.test('Status 201 — allowed on completed stage', () => pm.response.to.have.status(201));

item.name: [FRESH RFQ] TC-IT-10: Create + mark Lost with reason
[ANALYTICS] TC-IT-12: Win rate after Award + Lost
7 — Curated Demo Path (Meeting) [RUN THIS LIVE]
item.description:
**10 scenarios in a story-driven sequence. Run this folder in the meeting.**
Shows: creation → 11 stages → advance → mandatory reject → blocker reject → recovery → progress → cancel → freeze → permanence.
Time: 15-20 minutes.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "DEMO — Pressure Vessel Package for Offshore Platform",
  "client": "Saudi Aramco",
  "deadline": "2025-08-30",
  "owner": "Proposals Team A",
  "workflow_id": "{{workflow_long_id}}",
  "industry": "Oil & Gas",
  "country": "Saudi Arabia",
  "priority": "critical",
  "code_prefix": "IF"
}

item.name: DEMO-04: Try advance stage 2 WITHOUT mandatory → 422!

exec: pm.test('History preserved + rest skipped', () => pm.expect(completed + skipped).to.eql(stages.length));
